## Session conventions (recursive/emergent work)

This session is doing recursive or emergent work (taxonomy design, rubric
construction, debugging structure-discovery failures, or similar). The
following conventions apply. They are pointers, not full text — read the
referenced files when in doubt.

### When to call `hermes-ground` (external reality check)

- 3+ consecutive commits without a user-visible ship.
- A new scope class, taxonomy, or named architecture is proposed mid-session.
- 30+ minutes have elapsed since the last external check during emergent work.
- About to coin a noun-phrase label for something with no implementation at a path.
- A fix-attempt failed and you are tempted to ship a second fix without enumerating ≥3 alternative hypotheses.
- The user signals cognitive-bandwidth overflow ("I'm lost", "where are we", "are we drifting").
- Rubric-on-rubric depth ≥2.

Invoke: `~/bin/hermes-ground <briefing.md> --out /tmp/verdict.md`. Verdict is `GROUNDED`, `DRIFTING`, or `RE-PLAN`.

### Convention pointers (memory feedback files)

- `feedback_grounding_when_emergent.md` — call grounding on emergent recursion, not just on confusion.
- `feedback_calibrate_analysis_before_ship.md` — calibrate against synthetic ground-truth + cross-impl before claiming a result.
- `feedback_rubric_blind_passthrough_default.md` — every shippable artifact passes through a BLINDed rubric.
- `feedback_no_noun_phrase_before_file.md` — no "X System" / "Y Bundle" labels until a file exists at a path.

### Tool map

- `~/bin/hermes-ground` — fresh-context grounding agent (Sonnet via `claude --print`).
- `~/bin/hermes-ground-template.md` / `~/bin/hermes-ground-triggers.md` — briefing skeleton + trigger card.
- `~/bin/hermes-rubric-blinded` — scope-aware rubric pass-through (BLINDed).
- `~/Documents/projects/hermes-handbook/` — operational handbook (rubric pass-through pattern, debug-process rubric, specs).
- `~/bin/hermes-session-init` — this bootstrap (`--check` / `--print` / `--inject` / `--uninject`).

### The rule most often forgotten mid-session

**No noun-phrase label until a file exists at a path.** Names create the
illusion of completion. "We need an X Bundle" is wrong; "we need a CLAUDE.md
fragment listing the call conditions" is right. The label can be coined once
the file exists, not before.
